mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 6.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 6.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts indicate broad-based economic expansion, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of triggered windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a contraction in real consumer credit signals tightening financial conditions that suppress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50% of cases where FEDFUNDS rises and RSAFS contracts simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP expands the aggregate earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive forward equity returns over the next 20 trading days as valuation multiples stabilize.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 20-day equity returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 2.5 percent year-over-year growth, inflation expectations embed into longer-term discount rates, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 60% of windows where CPI growth surpasses 2.5 percent, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment combined with contracting consumer credit indicates a deleveraging household sector that will soon reduce consumption, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: The divergence between PAYEMS and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of historical instances, or the cross-product term loses significance in a multivariate model.

mechanism_narrative: During supply chain disruptions, a sustained drop in industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals capacity constraints that erode profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline when INDPRO dips below its trailing average, or the signal produces false breakdowns during normal cyclical dips.
